reasoning: Direct Israel-Iran strikes occurred in June 2025, followed by a shaky ceasefire as confirmed by multiple reports. While tensions remained extremely high in October 2025, with analysts forecasting potential new strikes, documented military activity in the latter half of the month (up to the October 26 cutoff) has focused on the Lebanese and Gaza fronts rather than direct strikes on Iranian soil. A regional de-escalation marker occurred on October 10, 2025, with a ceasefire between Israel and Hamas. Reports from the Institute for the Study of War (ISW) as late as October 24 indicate operations in Lebanon but make no mention of direct attacks on Iran or by Iran. Given only five days remain until the resolution date (Oct 31) and no strike has been recorded by Oct 26, the probability of the ceasefire being broken by a direct strike as defined in the criteria is low, though residual risk remains due to the volatile environment.
